name: Splunk Default Tools
description: Default tool collection for Splunk MCP Server
author: Splunk MCP Team

tools.name: get_info
tools.title: get_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive information about the Splunk instance. Retrieves system information including version, hardware specs, and operational status.

examples.name: Get Splunk Instance Info
examples.description: Retrieve comprehensive information about the Splunk instance

execution.template: | rest /services/server/info | fields version, build, serverName, os_name, cpu_arch, numberOfCores, physicalMemoryMB, licenseState, kvStoreStatus, server_roles, health_info

tools.name: get_indexes
tools.title: get_indexes
tools.description: Get a list of indexes from Splunk. Indexes are data repositories where machine data is stored and organized.

examples.name: List All Indexes
examples.description: Retrieve information about all available indexes

execution.template: | rest /services/data/indexes count=0 | fields title, disabled, currentDBSizeMB, totalEventCount, splunk_server

tools.name: get_index_info
tools.title: get_index_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific Splunk index. Returns comprehensive configuration and status information for the specified index.

index_name.description: Name of the index to get information about
index_name.validation_message: Index name can only contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), and hyphens (-)

examples.name: Get Main Index Info
examples.description: Retrieve detailed information about the main index

examples.name: Get Custom Index Info
examples.description: Retrieve information about a custom index

execution.template: | rest /services/data/indexes count=0 | search title=$index_name$ | fields title, disabled, currentDBSizeMB, totalEventCount, homePath, coldPath, thawedPath, maxDataSize, maxHotBuckets, maxWarmDBCount, datatype, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, bucketRebuildMemoryHint, splunk_server

tools.name: get_user_list
tools.title: get_user_list
tools.description: Get a list of users from Splunk. Retrieves information about all users including authentication details, roles, and account status.

examples.name: Get All Users
examples.description: Retrieve information about all users in the Splunk instance
examples.expected_use: User management, security audits, and access reviews

execution.template: | rest splunk_server=local /services/authentication/users count=0 | eval locked_out = if('locked-out'="1","true","false"), capabilities_count = mvcount(capabilities), last_successful_loginIso = if(isnull(last_successful_login) OR last_successful_login='', 'Never', strftime(last_successful_login, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")) | fields title, realname, email, roles, defaultApp, capabilities_count, locked_out, last_successful_login, last_successful_loginIso, type | rename title as username

tools.name: get_user_info
tools.title: get_user_info
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about the currently authenticated user including roles and permissions. Returns comprehensive user profile data for the current session.

examples.name: Get Current User Info
examples.description: Retrieve detailed information about the currently authenticated user
examples.expected_use: User profile verification, permission checking, and security context validation

execution.template: | rest splunk_server=local /services/authentication/current-context | eval locked_out = if('locked-out'="1" OR 'locked-out'="true", "true", "false"), capabilitiesCount = mvcount(capabilities), last_successful_loginIso = if(isnull(last_successful_login) OR last_successful_login="", "Never", strftime(last_successful_login, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")) | fields username, realname, email, roles, defaultApp, capabilitiesCount, locked_out, last_successful_login, last_successful_loginIso, type

tools.name: run_query
tools.title: run_query
tools.description: Execute a Splunk search query and return the results. This is the primary tool for running Splunk searches using SPL (Search Processing Language). Use this to retrieve log data, perform aggregations, analyze events, and extract insights from your Splunk environment.

query.validation_message: SPL query is required and limited to 5000 characters

examples.name: Simple Error Search
examples.description: Find error events in main index over last 24h

arguments.query: search index=main error | stats count by host

examples.name: Indexed Data Volume
examples.description: Get event counts per index in last hour

arguments.query: | tstats count where index=* earliest=-1h latest=now by index

tools.name: get_metadata
tools.title: get_metadata
tools.description: Retrieve metadata about hosts, sources, or sourcetypes across one or more indexes in the selected time window.

type.description: Metadata category to list (hosts, sources, or sourcetypes).

index.description: Index name or wildcard pattern (e.g. main, app_*, *).
index.validation_message: Index pattern may contain letters, numbers, underscore, dash, and * wildcard

examples.name: Hosts In Main
examples.description: List hosts present in main index

examples.name: Sourcetypes All Indexes
examples.description: List sourcetypes across all indexes (limited)

execution.template: | metadata type=$type$ index=$index$ | eval firstTimeIso = if(isnull(firstTime) OR firstTime='', 'Never', strftime(firstTime, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), lastTimeIso = if(isnull(lastTime) OR lastTime='', 'Never', strftime(lastTime, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), recentTimeIso = if(isnull(recentTime) OR recentTime='', 'Never', strftime(recentTime, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))

tools.name: get_kv_store_collections
tools.title: get_kv_store_collections
tools.description: Get KV Store collection statistics including size, count, and storage information. Retrieves comprehensive metrics about all KV Store collections in the Splunk instance.

examples.name: Get All KV Store Collections
examples.description: Retrieve statistics for all KV Store collections
examples.expected_use: KV Store monitoring, capacity planning, and performance analysis

execution.template:
| rest /services/server/introspection/kvstore/collectionstats | mvexpand data | spath input=data
| rex field=ns "^[^.]+\.(?<collection>.+)$"
| eval storageSize = coalesce(storageSize, size + totalIndexSize)
| eval storageSizeStr = case(
    isnull(storageSize), "0 B",
    storageSize < 1024, storageSize . " B",
    storageSize < 1024*1024, round(storageSize/1024, 2) . " KiB",
    storageSize < 1024*1024*1024, round(storageSize/1024/1024, 2) . " MiB",
    storageSize < 1024*1024*1024*1024, round(storageSize/1024/1024/1024, 2) . " GiB",
    true(), round(storageSize/1024/1024/1024/1024, 2) . " TiB"
)
| table collection size count storageSize storageSizeStr
| sort collection

tools.name: get_knowledge_objects
tools.title: get_knowledge_objects
tools.description: Retrieve Splunk knowledge objects by type. Supports various knowledge object types including saved searches, alerts, field extractions, lookups, macros, data models, and more.

type.description: Type of knowledge object to retrieve

enum.saved_searches: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), app='eai:acl.app' | fields name app search cron_schedule disabled dispatch.earliest_time dispatch.latest_time
enum.alerts: | rest "/services/saved/searches?search=alert_type%3D*" count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title) | fields name search alert_type actions cron_schedule disabled
enum.field_extractions: | rest /services/data/props/extractions count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name, title) | fields name type attribute stanza disabled description owner sharing app
enum.field_aliases: | rest /services/data/props/fieldaliases count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), source_key=stanza | fields name source_key alias_fields
enum.calculated_fields: | rest /services/data/props/calcfields count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), sourcetype=stanza, eval_expression=eval, field_name=field_name | fields name sourcetype eval_expression field_name
enum.lookups: | rest /services/data/lookup-table-files count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), app="eai:appName" | fields name app filename
enum.automatic_lookups: | rest /services/data/props/lookups count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), sourcetype=stanza | fields name sourcetype lookup_table fields_list
enum.lookup_transforms: | rest /services/data/transforms/lookups count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title) | fields name external_type filename fields_list
enum.macros: | rest /services/data/macros count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title) | rename args AS arguments | fields name definition arguments
enum.tags: | rest /services/search/tags count=0 | eval tag_name=coalesce(name,title) | fields tag_name canonical_name tag_name_aliases
enum.data_models: | rest /services/datamodel/model count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name, title) | fields name eai:acl.app eai:acl.owner eai:acl.sharing acceleration disabled
enum.workflow_actions: | rest /services/data/ui/workflow-actions count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title) | fields name label uri fields type
enum.views: | rest /services/data/ui/views count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), is_dashboard=isDashboard | fields name label is_dashboard
enum.panels: | rest /services/data/ui/panels count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name, title) | fields name eai:acl.app eai:acl.owner eai:acl.sharing definition
enum.apps: | rest splunk_server=local /services/apps/local count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title), visible=coalesce(visible,true()), enabled=if(disabled="0","true","false") | fields name version author description enabled visible
enum.mltk_algorithms: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/Splunk_ML_Toolkit/configs/conf-algos/ count=0 | eval name=coalesce(name,title) | fields name
enum.mltk_models: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/Splunk_ML_Toolkit/mltk/list_models count=0 | eval name=coalesce('mlspl:model_info.model_name', name) | eval target_variable=coalesce('mlspl:model_info.target_variable', target_variable) | eval feature_variables=coalesce('mlspl:model_info.feature_variables', feature_variables) | table name algo_name target_variable feature_variables  author owner app disabled updated model_file

examples.name: Get Saved Searches
examples.description: Retrieve all saved searches in the Splunk instance
examples.expected_use: Configuration management and saved search inventory

examples.name: Get Alerts
examples.description: Retrieve all alerts configured in the Splunk instance
examples.expected_use: Alert management and monitoring configuration review

examples.name: Get Lookup Tables
examples.description: Retrieve all lookup table files

examples.name: Get Data Models
examples.description: Retrieve all data models in the Splunk instance
examples.expected_use: Data model inventory and acceleration status review

tools.name: generate_spl
tools.title: generate_spl
tools.description: Generate SPL from natural language queries using Splunk AI Assistant.

prompt.description: Natural language query for generating SPL
prompt.validation_message: Prompt is required and limited to 1000 characters

chat_history.validation_message: Chat history is required and limited to 5000 characters

additional_context.description: Additional context to include in generated SPL

spl_only.description: If set to true, returns only generated SPL with no additional explanation

examples.name: Generate Top Users Query
examples.description: Generate SPL to find most active users

arguments.prompt: show me the top 5 users by activity
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We need SPL to list the top 5 most active users today."}]}

examples.name: Generate Performance Query
examples.description: Generate SPL for performance analysis

arguments.prompt: find slow queries that took more than 5 seconds
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"Prior chats were about identifying slow searches in prod."}]}

tools.name: explain_spl
tools.title: explain_spl
tools.description: Explain SPL queries in natural language using Splunk AI Assistant. Converts complex SPL commands into human-readable explanations.

spl.description: SPL query to explain in natural language
spl.validation_message: SPL query is required and limited to 5000 characters

chat_history.validation_message: Chat history is required and limited to 5000 characters

additional_context.description: Additional context to include in generated SPL

examples.name: Explain Error Search
examples.description: Get natural language explanation for an error search query

arguments.spl: search index=main error | stats count by host
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We ran an error search and want to document it."}]}

examples.expected_use: Understanding complex SPL queries and documenting search logic

examples.name: Explain Stats Query
examples.description: Explain a statistical aggregation query

arguments.spl: index=web | stats avg(response_time) max(response_time) by status_code
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"Earlier we discussed summarizing web response times."}]}

examples.name: Explain Complex Pipeline
examples.description: Get explanation for multi-stage SPL pipeline

arguments.spl: | tstats count where index=* by index | eval size_mb=round(count/1024/1024,2) | sort -size_mb
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We asked for help explaining a multi-stage tstats pipeline."}]}

examples.expected_use: Documentation and knowledge transfer for complex searches

tools.name: ask_splunk_question
tools.title: ask_splunk_question
tools.description: Ask natural language questions about Splunk using Splunk AI Assistant. Get explanations about Splunk commands, concepts, features, and best practices.

prompt.validation_message: Prompt is required and limited to 1000 characters

chat_history.validation_message: Chat history is required and limited to 5000 characters

additional_context.description: Additional context to include in generated SPL

examples.name: Ask About Splunk Commands
examples.description: Get information about specific Splunk commands

arguments.prompt: What does the inputlookup command do?
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We were reviewing lookup commands earlier."}]}

examples.expected_use: Learning Splunk commands and their functionality

examples.name: Ask About Data Models
examples.description: Get explanations about Splunk concepts

arguments.prompt: How do data models work in Splunk?
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We previously discussed data model accelerations."}]}

examples.name: Ask About Best Practices
examples.description: Get guidance on Splunk best practices

arguments.prompt: What are the best practices for indexing in Splunk?
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We were talking about indexing performance limits."}]}

examples.expected_use: Learning Splunk administration and optimization techniques

examples.name: Ask About Troubleshooting
examples.description: Get help with common Splunk issues

arguments.prompt: How do I troubleshoot slow search performance?
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We are experiencing slow searches in production."}]}

tools.name: optimize_spl
tools.title: optimize_spl
tools.description: Optimize SPL (Search Processing Language) queries using Splunk AI Assistant. Improves query performance, efficiency, and follows best practices.

spl.description: SPL query to optimize for better performance
spl.validation_message: SPL query is required and limited to 5000 characters

chat_history.validation_message: Chat history is required and limited to 5000 characters

additional_context.description: Additional context to include in generated SPL

examples.name: Optimize Basic Search
examples.description: Optimize a basic search query for better performance

arguments.spl: search index=* | stats count by host
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We previously asked about speeding up a simple host count search."}]}

examples.name: Optimize Complex Query
examples.description: Optimize a complex multi-stage query

arguments.spl: index=web | eval response_time_ms=response_time*1000 | where response_time_ms > 5000 | stats avg(response_time_ms) by status_code
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We discussed tuning a slow web response query."}]}

examples.expected_use: Performance tuning for complex analytical queries

examples.name: Optimize Join Query
examples.description: Optimize queries with joins or subsearches

arguments.spl: index=access | join host [search index=inventory | fields host, server_type] | stats count by server_type
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"Earlier we asked about slow join performance on host field."}]}

examples.name: Optimize Large Dataset Query
examples.description: Optimize queries that process large amounts of data

arguments.spl: search index=* earliest=-30d@d latest=@d | rex field=_raw "(?<user>\w+)" | stats dc(user) by index
arguments.chat_history: {"messages":[{"role":"developer","content":"You are a helpful assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"We were looking at long-running searches over 30 days of data."}]}

examples.expected_use: Performance optimization for large-scale data analysis

tools.name: run_saved_search
tools.title: run_saved_search
tools.description: Execute a Splunk saved search by name and return its results. Saved searches are pre-defined SPL queries stored in Splunk. Use get_knowledge_objects with type='saved_searches' to discover available saved searches and their app context. If the saved search name is unique across apps, the app is resolved automatically. If the same name exists in multiple apps, the alphabetically first app is used; pass the 'app' parameter to target a specific app. If the saved search contains placeholder tokens like $token_name$, pass replacement values as key=value pairs in the args parameter.

saved_search_name.description: Name of the saved search to execute
saved_search_name.validation_message: Saved search name is required, it cannot be blank and is limited to 500 characters.

args.description: Optional replacement arguments for saved search tokens
args.validation_message: args must be space-separated key="value" token assignments only (e.g. host="web01" sourcetype="syslog"). Pipe operators, brackets, backticks, semicolons, and newlines are not allowed in values.

earliest_time.description: Override the saved search's default start time (e.g., -24h, -7d, 2024-01-01T00:00:00). If omitted, uses the time range configured in the saved search.

latest_time.description: Override the saved search's default end time (e.g., now, -1h). If omitted, uses the time range configured in the saved search.

app.description: Splunk app context to run the saved search in (e.g., 'search', 'enterprise_security'). If omitted, the app is auto-resolved; when the name exists in multiple apps the alphabetically first app is used.
app.validation_message: App name may only contain letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, and dots.

examples.name: Run a saved search
examples.description: Execute a saved search by its name

examples.name: Run a saved search with arguments
examples.description: Execute a saved search with replacement token values
examples.expected_use: Running parameterized saved searches with specific filters
